segments.text: And Jacob called unto his sons, and said,
Gather yourselves together, that I may tell you that which shall befall you in the last days.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Gather yourselves together, and hear, ye sons of Jacob; and hearken unto Israel your father.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Reuben, thou art my firstborn, my might, and the beginning of my strength, the excellency of dignity, and the excellency of power:
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Unstable as water, thou shalt not excel; because thou wentest up to thy father's bed; then defiledst thou it: he went up to my couch.
segments.speaker: Jacob

footnotes: thou shalt not excel: Heb. do not thou excel
he went up to my couch: or, my couch is gone

segments.text: Simeon and Levi are brethren; instruments of cruelty are in their habitations.
segments.speaker: Jacob

footnotes: instruments of cruelty: or, their swords are weapons of violence
heading: Simeon and Levi

segments.text: O my soul, come not thou into their secret; unto their assembly, mine honour, be not thou united: for in their anger they slew a man, and in their selfwill they digged down a wall.
segments.speaker: Jacob

footnotes: digged down a wall: or, houghed oxen

segments.text: Cursed be their anger, for it was fierce; and their wrath, for it was cruel: I will divide them in Jacob, and scatter them in Israel.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Judah, thou art he whom thy brethren shall praise: thy hand shall be in the neck of thine enemies; thy father's children shall bow down before thee.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Judah is a lion's whelp: from the prey, my son, thou art gone up: he stooped down, he couched as a lion, and as an old lion; who shall rouse him up?
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: The sceptre shall not depart from Judah, nor a lawgiver from between his feet, until Shiloh come; and unto him shall the gathering of the people be.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Binding his foal unto the vine, and his ass's colt unto the choice vine; he washed his garments in wine, and his clothes in the blood of grapes:
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: His eyes shall be red with wine, and his teeth white with milk.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Zebulun shall dwell at the haven of the sea; and he shall be for an haven of ships; and his border shall be unto Zidon.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Issachar is a strong ass couching down between two burdens:
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: And he saw that rest was good, and the land that it was pleasant; and bowed his shoulder to bear, and became a servant unto tribute.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Dan shall judge his people, as one of the tribes of Israel.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Dan shall be a serpent by the way, an adder in the path, that biteth the horse heels, so that his rider shall fall backward.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: I have waited for thy salvation, O LORD.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Gad, a troop shall overcome him: but he shall overcome at the last.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Out of Asher his bread shall be fat, and he shall yield royal dainties.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Naphtali is a hind let loose: he giveth goodly words.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Joseph is a fruitful bough, even a fruitful bough by a well; whose branches run over the wall:
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: The archers have sorely grieved him, and shot at him, and hated him:
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: But his bow abode in strength, and the arms of his hands were made strong by the hands of the mighty God of Jacob; (from thence is the shepherd, the stone of Israel:)
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Even by the God of thy father, who shall help thee; and by the Almighty, who shall bless thee with blessings of heaven above, blessings of the deep that lieth under, blessings of the breasts, and of the womb:
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The blessings of thy father have prevailed above the blessings of my progenitors unto the utmost bound of the everlasting hills: they shall be on the head of Joseph, and on the crown of the head of him that was separate from his brethren.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Benjamin shall ravin as a wolf: in the morning he shall devour the prey, and at night he shall divide the spoil.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: All these are the twelve tribes of Israel: and this is it that their father spake unto them, and blessed them; every one according to his blessing he blessed them.
And he charged them, and said unto them,
I am to be gathered unto my people: bury me with my fathers in the cave that is in the field of Ephron the Hittite,
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: In the cave that is in the field of Machpelah, which is before Mamre, in the land of Canaan, which Abraham bought with the field of Ephron the Hittite for a possession of a buryingplace.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: There they buried Abraham and Sarah his wife; there they buried Isaac and Rebekah his wife; and there I buried Leah.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: The purchase of the field and of the cave that is therein was from the children of Heth.
And when Jacob had made an end of commanding his sons, he gathered up his feet into the bed, and yielded up the ghost, and was gathered unto his people.
